mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand and overall economic health, which typically translates to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in more than 55% of periods where RSAFS shows positive month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market and broader economic contraction, leading to lower corporate profits and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is rising while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated, it indicates a tightening monetary policy aimed at curbing inflation, which typically dampens economic activity and equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS has increased for 3 consecutive months AND CPIAUCSL annual growth > 3%
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS rises for 3 months and CPIAUCSL annual growth exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts (HOUST) during an economic expansion signals a potential slowdown in future construction activity and consumer confidence, leading to short-term negative market sentiment and S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where HOUST declines by more than 10% MoM during an expansion.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects a healthy manufacturing sector and overall economic expansion, which supports corporate earnings and positive equity market performance over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where INDPRO shows positive year-over-year growth.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in non-farm payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth indicates a weakening labor market, which can quickly translate into reduced consumer spending and lower corporate profits, negatively impacting stock prices over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth falls below 50,000 jobs per month
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in more than 45% of periods where PAYEMS 3-month average growth is below 50,000.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth (GDPC1) is strong but the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is low, it suggests an accommodative monetary environment supporting robust economic expansion, leading to strong equity market performance over a 60-day horizon.
regime_hint: early-to-mid cycle expansion with accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative in more than 30% of periods where GDPC1 annual growth exceeds 3% and FEDFUNDS is below 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid acceleration in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals potential for aggressive monetary tightening by the Federal Reserve, which can lead to higher discount rates and negative short-term equity market reactions over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth > 0.5% for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are positive in more than 55% of instances where CPIAUCSL MoM growth exceeds 0.5% for two consecutive months.
